attachment.content:
- frontend-design
- r-audit: Run an external audit of a pqverse R package (MiscMetabar, tidypq, comparpq). Creates independent test data, runs all exported functions, and produces an R script and a TODO report of warnings, errors, and recommendations. Use this skill to stress-test a package with edge cases (NAs, empty samples, zero-count taxa, malformed trees).
- r-build: Build the pkgdown documentation website and the R package source tarball. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build their R package, rebuild the pkgdown site, regenerate documentation, build the README, lint the package.
- r-check: Style, document, and run R CMD check on the current R package, then propose fixes for any issues found. Use this skill whenever the user wants to check, validate, or verify their R package, run R CMD check, fix NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs.
- r-pkg-bump-version: Bump an R package from a development version (X.Y.Z.9000) to a release version and finalize the NEWS.md heading. Use this skill whenever the user wants to bump the version of an R package for release, prepare DESCRIPTION + NEWS.md for CRAN, finalize the version before merging dev → master, or trigger Stage 8 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-merge-release: Merge an R package's dev branch into master, push, and bump dev to the next development cycle (X.Y.Z → X.Y.Z.9000). Use this skill after Stage 8 (version bump) when the user wants to finalize the release, merge dev into master, push to origin, start the next dev cycle, or trigger Stage 9 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-release: Full release pipeline for R packages: runs format, document, R CMD check (--as-cran), tests, optional coverage/audit/win-builder/rhub, CRAN extra checks, and builds the package. Supports --auto mode for autonomous runs that only stop for true ambiguity, with a resumable state file. Use this skill whenever the user wants to release a package, prepare for CRAN submission, run a pre-release check, do a full validation pipeline, or get a package ready for release. Also trigger when the user mentions 'release checklist', 'CRAN ready', 'prepare release'.
- r-stats
- r-test: Run the test suite and code coverage for the current R package, then propose new tests to improve coverage and fix failing tests. Use this skill whenever the user wants to run tests, check test coverage, fix failing tests, submit to Codecov.
- r-viz
- web-design-guidelines
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- posit-dev:critical-code-reviewer
- posit-dev:describe-design
- open-source:release-post
- open-source:create-release-checklist
- r-lib:testing-r-packages
- r-lib:cli
- r-lib:cran-extrachecks: Prepare R packages for CRAN submission by checking for common ad-hoc requirements not caught by devtools::check(). Use when: (1) Preparing a package for first CRAN release, (2) Preparing a package update for CRAN resubmission, (3) Reviewing a package to ensure CRAN compliance, (4) Responding to CRAN reviewer feedback. Covers documentation requirements, DESCRIPTION field standards, URL validation, examples, and administrative requirements.
- r-lib:lifecycle: Guidance for managing R package lifecycle according to tidyverse principles using the lifecycle package. Use when: (1) Setting up lifecycle infrastructure in a package, (2) Deprecating functions or arguments, (3) Renaming functions or arguments, (4) Superseding functions, (5) Marking functions as experimental, (6) Understanding lifecycle stages (stable, experimental, deprecated, superseded), or (7) Writing deprecation helpers for complex scenarios.
- r-lib:r-package-development
- r-lib:r-cli-app
- r-lib:mirai
- r-lib:alt-text
- quarto:brand-yml
- quarto:quarto-authoring
- quarto:alt-text
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.content:
1	# pqverse ROADMAP
2	
3	## How to read this file
4	
5	Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:
6	
7	| Priority | Meaning |
8	|---|---|
9	| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
10	| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
11	| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
12	| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |
13	
14	| Facility | Meaning |
15	|---|---|
16	| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
17	| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
18	| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |
19	
20	Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`
21	
22	> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.
23	
24	---
25	
26	# MiscMetabar
27	
28	## Architecture
29	
30	- **[High / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Full prototype available.
31	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:23–49; `arround_MiscMetabar/extend_phyloseq.qmd`)
32	
33	## Data I/O
34	
35	- **[High / easy]** `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` — export OTU table, sample data, taxonomy, and refseq to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission. Add Darwin Core DNA-derived-data extension compliance checks; optionally use `tidypq` for pre-export modifications.
36	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R`; `ROADMAP.md`:57–116)
37	
38	- **[High / easy]** `clean_pq()` gains a NA-cleanup option: replace `"NA"` and `"NA NA"` strings with true `<NA>` values (default `FALSE` to avoid breaking changes).
39	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:16–21)
40	
41	- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`).
42	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)
43	
44	- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
45	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)
46	
47	## Analysis / Statistics
48	
49	- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
50	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)
51	
52	- **[High / moderate]** `ancombc2_pq()` native wrapper — replaces dependence on microbiomeMarker (rejected; see Deferred section) for ANCOMBC2-based differential abundance.
53	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2492–2527)
54	
55	- **[High / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.
56	  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:52–54; https://github.com/bioscinema/Betadiag)
57	
58	- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for biomarker taxa using `lefser` + `ggtern`.
59	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1106–1179)
60	
61	- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary + diamond plots for 3–4 group compositions — port Mahendra Mariadassou's implementation, simplify with `psmelt`. **Requires permission from M. Mariadassou before use.**
62	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1182–1525)
63	
64	- **[Medium / moderate]** Correlation iconography — wrappers around `HelpersMG::IC_threshold_matrix()` and `IC_correlation_simplify()`.
65	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:955–1005)
66	
67	- **[Medium / moderate]** GDM (Generalized Dissimilarity Modelling) wrapper using `gdm::formatsitepair()` + `gdm.varImp()`.
68	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1938–2004)
69	
70	- **[Medium / moderate]** TITAN2 threshold indicator analysis wrapper.
71	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2031–2051)
72	
73	- **[Medium / hard]** PLS-DA / sPLS-DA via `mixOmics`.
74	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1011–1089)
75	
76	- **[Low / moderate]** Hill accumulation with `divent` (replaces iNEXT approach) + proper singleton handling. **Action required: discuss with É. Marcon.**
77	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2134–2143)
78	
79	- **[High / moderate]** Harmonize parameter names across all functions: `fact`, `bifactor`, `by.fact`, `merge_sample_by`, `modality`, `variable`, `var_to_test` — in progress.
80	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)
81	
82	- **[Medium / easy]** `assign_sintax()` gains a vote-based consensus method alongside / instead of the `lcaout` output (e.g. majority-vote at configurable threshold).
83	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)
84	
85	- **[Medium / moderate]** Post-clustering via ABGD / ASAP (when available on CRAN) — see `arround_MiscMetabar/delimtools_trying.R` for a prototype.
86	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)
87	
88	- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate `Rsearch` bioinfo functions: `plot_read_quality()`, `plot_ee_rate_dist()`, `vs_fastx_uniques()`, `vs_uchime_ref()`, `vs_uchime_denovo()`.
89	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme; https://cassandrahjo.github.io/Rsearch/)
90	
91	- **[Medium / moderate]** `resolve_vector_ranks()` gains a bitscore threshold parameter (top 2% of best hit) and a majority-vote threshold (e.g. 85%) inspired by MEGAN6 LCA filtering.
92	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)
93	
94	- **[Medium / moderate]** Robust Aitchison PCA (rCLR + matrix completion, deicode-style) — partial implementation via `rpca::rpca()`; see also upcoming `vegan` PR.
95	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
96	
97	- **[Medium / moderate]** ALDEx2 scale-uncertainty sensitivity analysis: `aldex.senAnalysis()` + `plotGamma()` for gamma-range exploration.
98	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices/diff abundance)
99	
100	- **[Medium / moderate]** LDA Effect Size (LefSe) wrapper via `lefser` package.
101	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)
102	
103	- **[Medium / moderate]** `adiv::dbMANOVAspecies()` indicator-species method (Pavoine 2020).
104	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)
105	
106	- **[Medium / moderate]** Zeta-diversity wrappers via `zetadiv` package (Hui & McGeoch 2014).
107	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
108	
109	- **[Medium / moderate]** `DivNet` + `breakaway` for bias-corrected alpha-diversity estimation.
110	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
111	
112	- **[Medium / moderate]** aPCoA (adjusted PCoA, confounder-corrected) via MicrobiomeStat implementation.
113	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)
114	
115	- **[Medium / moderate]** dbMEM (distance-based Moran's Eigenvector Maps) for spatial variation partitioning.
116	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)
117	
118	- **[Low / easy]** `indicspecies` additional association/indicator functions (see indval, phi, A, B).
119	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
120	
121	- **[Low / moderate]** NST + iCAMP for null-model community-assembly analysis (stochastic vs. deterministic partitioning).
122	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
123	
124	- **[Low / moderate]** Proportionality analysis (`MicEco::proportionality()`) as a CoDA-friendly co-occurrence metric.
125	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
126	
127	- **[Low / moderate]** `coda4microbiome` and `philr` integration for isometric log-ratio–based analyses.
128	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
129	
130	- **[Low / moderate]** Welch MANOVA for robust multivariate group comparison.
131	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
132	
133	- **[Low / moderate]** SparseMCMM compositional mediation analysis.
134	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
135	
136	- **[Low / moderate]** Spike-in analysis for absolute abundance quantification correction.
137	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
138	
139	- **[Low / hard]** `TreeSummarizedExperiment` integration as an alternative data container.
140	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
141	
142	- **[Low / moderate]** rANOMALY + psadd inspiration for normalisation/transformation functions — review their approaches for additional transform options.
143	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — normalisation)
144	
145	- **[Low / moderate]** tidyomics — assess integration/compatibility with the tidyomics ecosystem.
146	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
147	
148	- **[Low / moderate]** Simulate communities utility using `miaSim` (Hubbell/LV models) for test data generation + function examples for `dist_pos_control`, `subset_taxa_tax_control`.
149	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; vignettes)
150	
151	- **[Low / easy]** More vignette examples using `microbiomeDataSets` + `miaSim` + Grinder / SparseDOSSA2 for realistic simulated data.
152	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)
153	
154	- **[Low / easy]** Vignette on microbiome data structure characteristics: count-based, compositional, zero-inflated, high-dimensional, tree-structured (Peterson et al. 2024 Ann. Rev. Statistics).
155	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)
156	
157	- **[Low / easy]** Vignette on zero-inflated distributions in microbiome data with recommended modelling approaches.
158	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, vignettes)
159	
160	## Visualisation
161	
162	- **[High / easy]** `reshape_ggplot()` — auto-wrap plot titles, subtitles, and axis labels using `stringr::str_wrap()`. Full prototype available.
163	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2063–2127)
164	
165	- **[Medium / moderate]** `wheat_plot_gg()` — wheat / violin hybrid plot for compositional data. Prototype available.
166	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2285–2391)
167	
168	- **[Medium / moderate]** Comet plot for 2-variable and 3-variable comparisons (inspired by zanarmstrong). Two prototypes available.
169	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2146–2281)
170	
171	- **[Low / moderate]** Color palette derived from geographic location via `earthtones`; also option to pull color from taxon photo URL (`wt_photos_gallery_pq`). Fix upstream latitude-invariance bug before integrating.
172	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2289–2338)
173	
174	- **[Medium / easy]** Volcano plot for differential abundance results (DESeq2 / ANCOMBC2 output): log₂FC vs −log₁₀(padj), directional coloring (Up / Down / Not DA).
175	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)
176	
177	- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for diversity decomposition per Ricotta & Pavoine 2024 using `adiv` package (distinct from the compositional 3/4-group ternary plots already planned).
178	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — plot; https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolind.2024.111...)
179	
180	- **[Medium / easy]** `envfit()` integration from vegan inside ordination functions (`biplot_pq()`, `pcoa_pq()`) to overlay environmental vectors.
181	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)
182	
183	- **[Medium / easy]** `plot_overview_pq()` — single-call function producing basic alpha + beta diversity figures given a phyloseq object and a grouping factor.
184	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)
185	
186	## Bioinfo wrappers
187	
188	- **[High / easy]** `fastp()` quality-control + adapter-detection wrapper for FASTQ files. Draft implementation ready.
189	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/fastp.R`)

211	
212	- **[Low / hard]** Sequence similarity networks / NSC reclustering per Forster et al. 2019.
213	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2576–2610)
214	
215	- **[Medium / moderate]** Function to build a `phylo` tree from a taxonomy table — inspired by `TreeSummarizedExperiment::tree_toTree()` and `Rsearch::taxonomy_tree()`.
216	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)
217

219	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

221	- **[Medium / moderate]** MMseqs2 integration for taxonomic assignment (`easy-taxonomy` mode) — high performance for marine / short-read metabarcoding.

227	- **[Low / hard]** FunVIP + Sativa + tronko for phylogeny-guided taxonomic assignment and post-assignment verification; also `phylotax`.
228	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)

Remaining work: fill `_targets.R` primer/refseq placeholders, complete `02_alpha_diversity.qmd` / `03_beta_diversity.qmd` factor-of-interest params, populate `NEWS.md`, ship Docker image.

449	
450	## Submission order
451	
452	The order is dictated by the dependency graph. Each package must be on CRAN before downstream dependents can submit.
453	
454	| # | Package | Version | Bioconptron in Depends/Imports? | Non-CRAN Suggests? | Blockers | Readiness |
455	|---|---------|---------|------------------------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|
456	| 1 | greenAlgoR | 0.1.2 | No | No | Minor | High |
457	| 2 | tidypq | 0.1.1 | phyloseq (Depends) | Biostrings, dada2 | phyloseq risk | Medium-High |
458	| 3 | taxinfo | 0.1.2 | phyloseq (Imports) | gbif.range | LazyData, gbif.range guard | Medium |
459	| 4 | comparpq | 0.1.3 | phyloseq (Depends), Biostrings (Imports) | Multiple Bioc | Quarto vignette, Biostrings in Imports | Medium-Low |
460	| 5 | dbpq | 0.0.0.9000 | Biostrings (Imports) | dada2 | Version bump, Biostrings → Suggests | Low |
461	| 6 | MiscMetabar | 0.16.1.9000 | phyloseq, dada2 (Depends) | None critical | Already on CRAN; .html vignette cleanup | On CRAN |
462	| 7 | pqverse | 0.1.0 | None direct | None | ALL Imports not on CRAN yet | Very Low |
463	
464	> MiscMetabar is already on CRAN and is listed last because it needs only maintenance fixes. pqverse must wait for every other package to clear CRAN first.
465

file.content:
# pqverse ROADMAP

## How to read this file

Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:

| Priority | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |

| Facility | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |

Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`

> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.

---

# MiscMetabar

## Architecture

- **[High / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Full prototype available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:23–49; `arround_MiscMetabar/extend_phyloseq.qmd`)

## Data I/O

- **[High / easy]** `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` — export OTU table, sample data, taxonomy, and refseq to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission. Add Darwin Core DNA-derived-data extension compliance checks; optionally use `tidypq` for pre-export modifications.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R`; `ROADMAP.md`:57–116)

- **[High / easy]** `clean_pq()` gains a NA-cleanup option: replace `"NA"` and `"NA NA"` strings with true `<NA>` values (default `FALSE` to avoid breaking changes).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:16–21)

- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)

- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)

## Analysis / Statistics

- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)

- **[High / moderate]** `ancombc2_pq()` native wrapper — replaces dependence on microbiomeMarker (rejected; see Deferred section) for ANCOMBC2-based differential abundance.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2492–2527)

- **[High / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:52–54; https://github.com/bioscinema/Betadiag)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for biomarker taxa using `lefser` + `ggtern`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1106–1179)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary + diamond plots for 3–4 group compositions — port Mahendra Mariadassou's implementation, simplify with `psmelt`. **Requires permission from M. Mariadassou before use.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1182–1525)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Correlation iconography — wrappers around `HelpersMG::IC_threshold_matrix()` and `IC_correlation_simplify()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:955–1005)

- **[Medium / moderate]** GDM (Generalized Dissimilarity Modelling) wrapper using `gdm::formatsitepair()` + `gdm.varImp()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1938–2004)

- **[Medium / moderate]** TITAN2 threshold indicator analysis wrapper.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2031–2051)

- **[Medium / hard]** PLS-DA / sPLS-DA via `mixOmics`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1011–1089)

- **[Low / moderate]** Hill accumulation with `divent` (replaces iNEXT approach) + proper singleton handling. **Action required: discuss with É. Marcon.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2134–2143)

- **[High / moderate]** Harmonize parameter names across all functions: `fact`, `bifactor`, `by.fact`, `merge_sample_by`, `modality`, `variable`, `var_to_test` — in progress.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / easy]** `assign_sintax()` gains a vote-based consensus method alongside / instead of the `lcaout` output (e.g. majority-vote at configurable threshold).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Post-clustering via ABGD / ASAP (when available on CRAN) — see `arround_MiscMetabar/delimtools_trying.R` for a prototype.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate `Rsearch` bioinfo functions: `plot_read_quality()`, `plot_ee_rate_dist()`, `vs_fastx_uniques()`, `vs_uchime_ref()`, `vs_uchime_denovo()`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme; https://cassandrahjo.github.io/Rsearch/)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `resolve_vector_ranks()` gains a bitscore threshold parameter (top 2% of best hit) and a majority-vote threshold (e.g. 85%) inspired by MEGAN6 LCA filtering.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — taxonomy)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Robust Aitchison PCA (rCLR + matrix completion, deicode-style) — partial implementation via `rpca::rpca()`; see also upcoming `vegan` PR.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** ALDEx2 scale-uncertainty sensitivity analysis: `aldex.senAnalysis()` + `plotGamma()` for gamma-range exploration.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices/diff abundance)

- **[Medium / moderate]** LDA Effect Size (LefSe) wrapper via `lefser` package.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `adiv::dbMANOVAspecies()` indicator-species method (Pavoine 2020).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — espèces indicatrices)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Zeta-diversity wrappers via `zetadiv` package (Hui & McGeoch 2014).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `DivNet` + `breakaway` for bias-corrected alpha-diversity estimation.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

- **[Medium / moderate]** aPCoA (adjusted PCoA, confounder-corrected) via MicrobiomeStat implementation.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)

- **[Medium / moderate]** dbMEM (distance-based Moran's Eigenvector Maps) for spatial variation partitioning.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — multivariate)

# Proposed new packages

## netaipq — Networks and ML for phyloseq

The next new package to develop in the pqverse. Wraps network inference,
bipartite visualisation, and machine-learning methods for phyloseq objects.

- **[High / moderate]** Bipartite-network visualisation — samples and taxa as nodes, edges from the OTU table, inspired by Taudiere et al. 2018 Fig. 2 and vda-lab/snowflake. ggplot2 only (no htmlwidgets). Supports sample-based and taxa-based metrics as aesthetics.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md` previous version; https://gitlab.com/vda-lab/snowflake)

- **[High / hard]** DAGITTY causal DAG integration — build and test causal models with `dagitty::localTests()` using phyloseq data.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:218–270)

- **[High / hard]** Co-occurrence networks via `cooccur` (Veech 2013 probabilistic model). Note: bug when abundance data is passed — needs fix.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:281–354)

- **[Medium / hard]** `magma_pq()` — rMAGMA microbial association network inference. Full prototype available.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:430–494)

- **[Medium / hard]** NetCoMi wrapper — taxon and sample-level networks, Aitchison distance-based.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:497–659)

- **[Medium / hard]** `CompoCor::SparCEV` compositional correlation with environmental variable.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:357–380)

- **[Medium / hard]** `ggClusterNet::network.2` / `network.i` wrappers.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:384–427)

- **[Medium / hard]** `microeco::trans_network` wrapper.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:662–696)

  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:702–730)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Random Forest wrappers: Boruta variable importance (`Boruta::Boruta`), plain `randomForest`, and `rfPermute` (Alexis Carteron variant) — all operating on `psmelt_samples_pq` output.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:748–891)

- **[Medium / hard]** MiRKAT microbiome-outcome association using UniFrac / GUniFrac kernels.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:894–934)

- **[Medium / moderate]** SHAP value plots after random forest for variable importance interpretation (`iml`, `fastshap`, or `DALEX`).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Medium / hard]** EndDED for environmental filtering of ecological interaction networks (disentangle direct vs. environment-driven associations).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Medium / hard]** qcmi integration for quantitative co-occurrence networks.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme — network)

- **[Low / hard]** JSDMs / gllvm / sjSDM / MrIML for model-based co-occurrence inference (Bayesian framework, accounts for environmental confounders).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme)

## Ecosystem siblings (not pqverse packages, but co-developed here)

- **enasubmit** — R package for ENA sequence data submission. Already scaffolded (`checklist.R`, `submit.R`, `taxonomy.R`, vignette). Located at `arround_MiscMetabar/enasubmit/`.

- **bioinfo.starter** — `targets`-based metabarcoding pipeline template. Located at `arround_MiscMetabar/bioinfo.starter/`. Remaining work: fill `_targets.R` primer/refseq placeholders, complete `02_alpha_diversity.qmd` / `03_beta_diversity.qmd` factor-of-interest params, populate `NEWS.md`, ship Docker image.

- **docker_starter** and **starter_folder** rest on the pqverse package

---

# Cross-cutting concerns

- **Hill diversity convention** — all new Hill-based functions must use `divent::div_hill()` with `estimator = "UnveilJ"` as default (migration completed in MiscMetabar 0.15.1 and comparpq 0.2.1; deprecate `scales`/`hill_scales` params in favour of `q`).

- **Windows platform** — 13+ MiscMetabar functions are documented as non-working on Windows (`build_phytree_pq`, `cutadapt_remove_primers`, `krona`, `venn_pq`, etc.). Consider improving Docker workflow documentation as the primary mitigation.

- **Pending external contacts:**
  - **Mahendra Mariadassou** — permission to port ternary/diamond plot implementation into MiscMetabar.
  - **Éric Marcon** — discuss `divent`-based Hill accumulation and singleton handling approach.

- **Dataset licensing** — before bundling FungalTraits, TAXREFv18, or BDC_18 into any `inst/extdata`, verify that the respective licenses permit redistribution inside an R package.

---

# CRAN Submission Roadmap

> Goal: submit every pqverse package to CRAN, in dependency-first order.

## Submission order

The order is dictated by the dependency graph. Each package must be on CRAN before downstream dependents can submit.

| # | Package | Version | Bioconptron in Depends/Imports? | Non-CRAN Suggests? | Blockers | Readiness |
|---|---------|---------|------------------------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | greenAlgoR | 0.1.2 | No | No | Minor | High |
| 2 | tidypq | 0.1.1 | phyloseq (Depends) | Biostrings, dada2 | phyloseq risk | Medium-High |
| 3 | taxinfo | 0.1.2 | phyloseq (Imports) | gbif.range | LazyData, gbif.range guard | Medium |
| 4 | comparpq | 0.1.3 | phyloseq (Depends), Biostrings (Imports) | Multiple Bioc | Quarto vignette, Biostrings in Imports | Medium-Low |
| 5 | dbpq | 0.0.0.9000 | Biostrings (Imports) | dada2 | Version bump, Biostrings → Suggests | Low |
| 6 | MiscMetabar | 0.16.1.9000 | phyloseq, dada2 (Depends) | None critical | Already on CRAN; .html vignette cleanup | On CRAN |
| 7 | pqverse | 0.1.0 | None direct | None | ALL Imports not on CRAN yet | Very Low |

> MiscMetabar is already on CRAN and is listed last because it needs only maintenance fixes. pqverse must wait for every other package to clear CRAN first.

Package: dbpq
Title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
Version: 0.0.0.9000
Authors@R:
    person("Adrien", "Taudière", , "adrien.taudiere@zaclys.net", role = c("aut", "cre", "cph"),
           comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-1088-1182"))
Description: Download, format, summarize, and modify FASTA reference
    databases used for taxonomic assignment in metabarcoding pipelines.
    Supports major databases ('UNITE', 'SILVA', 'PR2', 'Greengenes2', 'RDP',
    'MIDORI2', 'BOLD', 'Diat.barcode', 'MaarjAM', 'Eukaryome') and converts
    between taxonomy header formats ('dada2', 'SINTAX', 'Greengenes2'). Part
    of the 'pqverse' ecosystem.
License: MIT + file LICENSE
URL: https://github.com/adrientaudiere/dbpq
BugReports: https://github.com/adrientaudiere/dbpq/issues
Depends:

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Package Overview

**dbpq** is an R package for managing FASTA reference databases used in taxonomic assignment for metabarcoding. It provides tools to download, format, summarize, and modify databases. Part of the pqverse ecosystem.

## Common Commands

```bash
# Run code with loaded package
Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(); code"

# Run all tests
Rscript -e "devtools::test()"

# Run tests for files starting with {name}
Rscript -e "devtools::test(filter = '^{name}')"

# Run tests for R/{name}.R
Rscript -e "devtools::test_active_file('R/{name}.R')"

# Generate documentation
Rscript -e "devtools::document()"

# Full package check
Rscript -e "devtools::check()"
```

## Architecture

### Key Modules

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `dbpq-package.R` | Package documentation and imports |
| `utils.R` | Internal helpers (is_gzipped, read_lines_db, get_file_extension) |
| `download.R` | download_*_db() functions for major databases |
| `format.R` | format2dada2(), format2sintax(), format2dada2_species() |
| `summarize.R` | count_seq_db(), count_pattern_db(), list_ranks_db(), summarize_db() |
| `modify.R` | filter_db(), cutadapt_rm_primers_db() |

### Function Naming Convention

- `{verb}_db()` for actions on databases (e.g., `filter_db()`, `summarize_db()`)
- `{verb}_{what}_db()` for specific operations (e.g., `count_pattern_db()`, `count_seq_db()`)
- `download_{source}_db()` for download functions
- `format2{target}()` for format conversion functions

### Design Principles

- Accept both plain and gzip FASTA files throughout
- Silent by default, `verbose = TRUE` for messages
- No dependency on MiscMetabar (standalone package)
- Use Biostrings for FASTA I/O

## Coding Conventions

- Use base pipe (`|>`) not magrittr (`%>%`)
- Use `\() ...` for single-line anonymous functions, `function() {...}` otherwise
- Tests for `R/{name}.R` go in `tests/testthat/test-{name}.R`

1	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
2	# Internal download helper
3	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
4	
5	#' Download a file with progress and validation
6	#'
7	#' @param url The URL to download from.
8	#' @param dest_path The local file path to save to.
9	#' @param verbose Print progress messages.
10	#'
11	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
12	#' @keywords internal
13	download_file <- function(url, dest_path, verbose = TRUE) {
14	  if (verbose) {
15	    message("Downloading from:\n  ", url)
16	    message("Saving to:\n  ", dest_path)
17	  }
18	
19	  tryCatch(
20	    {
21	      utils::download.file(
22	        url,
23	        destfile = dest_path,
24	        mode = "wb",
25	        quiet = !verbose
26	      )
27	    },
28	    error = function(e) {
29	      if (file.exists(dest_path)) {
30	        unlink(dest_path)
31	      }
32	      stop(
33	        "Download failed. Check your internet connection and that the URL ",
34	        "is still valid:\n  ",
35	        url,
36	        "\nOriginal error: ",
37	        conditionMessage(e),
38	        call. = FALSE
39	      )
40	    }
41	  )
42	
43	  if (!file.exists(dest_path) || file.size(dest_path) == 0) {
44	    if (file.exists(dest_path)) {
45	      unlink(dest_path)
46	    }
47	    stop(
48	      "Download produced an empty file. The URL may be invalid:\n  ",
49	      url,
50	      call. = FALSE
51	    )
52	  }
53	
54	  if (verbose) {
55	    size_mb <- round(file.size(dest_path) / 1024^2, 1)
56	    message("Download complete (", size_mb, " MB)")
57	  }
58	
59	  invisible(dest_path)
60	}
61	
62	
63	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
64	# UNITE
65	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
66	
67	#' Download a UNITE reference database
68	#'
69	#' @description
70	#' Downloads the UNITE fungal ITS database for taxonomic assignment from
71	#' the UNITE DOI repository (PlutoF/Zenodo). Provides both "dynamic"
72	#' (including singletons) and "static" versions, for fungi or all eukaryotes.
73	#'
74	#' UNITE general FASTA releases use the `k__`/`p__` taxonomy format and
75	#' are compatible with dada2, VSEARCH, and other classifiers after
76	#' reformatting with [format2dada2()] or [format2sintax()].
77	#'
78	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
79	#'   downloaded file.
80	#' @param type (Character, default `"dynamic"`) One of `"dynamic"` or
81	#'   `"static"`. Dynamic files include singletons. Only used when
82	#'   `taxonomic_format = "default"`. Note: as of UNITE v10.0, separate
83	#'   static/dynamic archives are not available for `taxon_group = "fungi"`;
84	#'   both options download the same archive.
85	#' @param taxon_group (Character, default `"fungi"`) One of `"fungi"` or
86	#'   `"eukaryotes"`.
87	#' @param version (Character, default `"10.0"`) UNITE version. Use `"10.0"`
88	#'   for the 2024 release.
89	#' @param taxonomic_format (Character, default `"default"`) One of
90	#'   `"default"` or `"sintax"`. When `"default"`, downloads the general
91	#'   FASTA release (`.tgz` archive with `k__`/`p__` taxonomy). When
92	#'   `"sintax"`, downloads a single FASTA file already formatted for
93	#'   VSEARCH SINTAX classification (with `tax=d:...` headers).
94	#' @param doi (Character) If provided, overrides version-based URL
95	#'   construction with a direct DOI download. Useful for older or
96	#'   alternative releases.
97	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
98	#'
99	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
100	#' @export
101	#' @author Adrien Taudière
102	#' @details
103	#' When `taxonomic_format = "default"`: since UNITE v10.0, the `.tgz`
104	#' archive contains all clustering variants (dynamic, 97%, 99%) bundled
105	#' together. After downloading, extract the archive and select the
106	#' appropriate FASTA file.
107	#'
108	#' When `taxonomic_format = "sintax"`: the downloaded `.gz` file is a
109	#' single FASTA with SINTAX-ready taxonomy headers. This file can be used
110	#' directly with `vsearch --sintax`.
111	#'
112	#' The S3 download URLs use opaque UUIDs that change between releases.
113	#' If the automatic URL fails, visit
114	#' <https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php> to find the current DOI and pass
115	#' it via the `doi` parameter, or download manually.
116	#'
117	#' Please cite UNITE: Abarenkov K et al. (2024) UNITE general FASTA
118	#' release for Fungi. UNITE Community.
119	#' \doi{10.15156/BIO/2959336}
120	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()]
121	#' @examples
122	#' \dontrun{
123	#' # Download UNITE v10.0 default (general FASTA archive) for fungi
124	#' download_unite_db(dest_dir = "databases")
125	#'
126	#' # Download UNITE v10.0 SINTAX-formatted for fungi
127	#' download_unite_db(dest_dir = "databases", taxonomic_format = "sintax")
128	#'
129	#' # Download for all eukaryotes in SINTAX format
130	#' download_unite_db(
131	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
132	#'   taxon_group = "eukaryotes",
133	#'   taxonomic_format = "sintax"
134	#' )
135	#' }
136	download_unite_db <- function(
137	  dest_dir = ".",
138	  type = c("dynamic", "static"),
139	  taxon_group = c("fungi", "eukaryotes"),
140	  version = "10.0",
141	  taxonomic_format = c("default", "sintax"),
142	  doi = NULL,
143	  verbose = TRUE
144	) {
145	  type <- match.arg(type)
146	  taxon_group <- match.arg(taxon_group)
147	  taxonomic_format <- match.arg(taxonomic_format)
148	
149	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
150	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
151	  }
152	
153	  if (!is.null(doi)) {
154	    # User-provided DOI: resolve to download URL
155	    url <- paste0("https://doi.org/", sub("^https?://doi.org/", "", doi))
156	    ext <- ifelse(taxonomic_format == "sintax", ".gz", ".tgz")
157	    dest_file <- file.path(
158	      dest_dir,
159	      paste0("UNITE_", type, "_", taxon_group, ext)
160	    )
161	    message(
162	      "Using user-provided DOI. You may need to follow redirects manually.\n",
163	      "Consider downloading from https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php"
164	    )
165	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
166	    return(invisible(dest_file))
167	  }
168	
169	  if (taxonomic_format == "sintax") {
170	    # Known S3 download URLs for UNITE v10.0 SINTAX FASTA release
171	    # Source: nf-core/ampliseq ref_databases.config (sintax_ref_taxonomy)
172	    # Note: S3 UUIDs are opaque and change between releases
173	    sintax_urls <- list(
174	      fungi = paste0(
175	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
176	        "b27cffec-1e7d-4584-93d3-12add9fa180b.gz"
177	      ),
178	      eukaryotes = paste0(
179	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
180	        "6f19ddb6-1ac0-4834-a74c-b639688878a4.gz"
181	      )
182	    )
183	
184	    url <- sintax_urls[[taxon_group]]
185	
186	    if (is.null(url)) {
187	      stop(
188	        "No known SINTAX download URL for UNITE version ",
189	        version,
190	        " (",
191	        taxon_group,
192	        ").\n",
193	        "Visit https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php for manual download.",
194	        call. = FALSE
195	      )
196	    }
197	
198	    dest_file <- file.path(
199	      dest_dir,
200	      paste0(
201	        "sh_general_release_sintax_",
202	        ifelse(taxon_group == "fungi", "s_", "s_all_"),
203	        gsub("\\.", "", version),
204	        ".fasta.gz"
205	      )
206	    )
207	
208	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
209	
210	    if (verbose) {
211	      message(
212	        "UNITE SINTAX database saved as: ",
213	        dest_file,
214	        "\n",
215	        "This file can be used directly with: ",
216	        "vsearch --sintax reads.fasta --db ",
217	        dest_file,
218	        " --tabbedout out.txt --sintax_cutoff 0.8"
219	      )
220	    }
221	
222	    return(invisible(dest_file))
223	  }
224	
225	  # Known S3 download URLs for UNITE v10.0 general FASTA release
226	
227	  # Source: nf-core/ampliseq ref_databases.config
228	  # The .tgz archives contain dynamic, 97%, and 99% clustering variants
229	  # Note: S3 UUIDs are opaque and change between releases
230	  unite_urls <- list(
231	    fungi = list(
232	      dynamic = paste0(
233	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
234	        "d18aa648-3f4c-4f46-84d4-c8c5d48439ba.tgz"
235	      ),
236	      static = paste0(
237	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
238	        "d18aa648-3f4c-4f46-84d4-c8c5d48439ba.tgz"
239	      )
240	    ),
241	    eukaryotes = list(
242	      dynamic = paste0(
243	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
244	        "1dda2021-4893-4f2f-b50e-87bfea795267.tgz"
245	      ),
246	      static = paste0(
247	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
248	        "1dda2021-4893-4f2f-b50e-87bfea795267.tgz"
249	      )
250	    )
251	  )
252	
253	  if (taxon_group == "fungi" && type == "static") {
254	    message(
255	      "Note: UNITE v10.0 does not ship separate static/dynamic archives for ",
256	      "fungi. Downloading the single available fungi archive."
257	    )
258	  }
259	
260	  url <- unite_urls[[taxon_group]][[type]]
261	
262	  if (is.null(url)) {
263	    stop(
264	      "No known download URL for UNITE version ",
265	      version,
266	      " (",
267	      type,
268	      ", ",
269	      taxon_group,
270	      ").\n",
271	      "Visit https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php for manual download.",
272	      call. = FALSE
273	    )
274	  }
275	
276	  dest_file <- file.path(
277	    dest_dir,
278	    paste0(
279	      "sh_general_release_",
280	      type,
281	      "_",
282	      ifelse(taxon_group == "fungi", "s_", "s_all_"),
283	      gsub("\\.", "", version),
284	      ".tgz"
285	    )
286	  )
287	
288	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
289	
290	  if (verbose) {
291	    message(
292	      "UNITE database saved as: ",
293	      dest_file,
294	      "\n",
295	      "Extract with: untar('",
296	      dest_file,
297	      "', exdir = '",
298	      dest_dir,
299	      "')"
300	    )
301	  }
302	
303	  invisible(dest_file)
304	}
305	
306	
307	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
308	# SILVA
309	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
310	
311	#' Download a SILVA reference database NR99 version
312	#'
313	#' @description
314	#' Downloads the SILVA ribosomal RNA database (16S/18S/23S/28S). By default,
315	#' downloads the dada2-formatted training sets from the official arb-silva.de
316	#' DADA2 release (available for both SSU and LSU). Can also produce a
317	#' SINTAX-formatted database (converted locally from the dada2 trainset) or
318	#' download the raw SILVA NR99 export.
319	#' 
320	#' For the PARC version (all sequences, not clustered like NR99), see 
321	#' `dada2:::makeSpeciesFasta_Silva()` on the manually downloaded FASTA file.
322	#'
323	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
324	#'   downloaded file.
325	#' @param version (Character, default `"138.2"`) SILVA version number. Only
326	#'   the current SILVA release is hosted at the arb-silva DADA2 path used by
327	#'   the dada2/dada2_species/sintax formats.
328	#' @param target (Character, default `"SSU"`) One of `"SSU"` or `"LSU"`.
329	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
330	#'   - `"dada2"`: dada2-formatted `toSpecies` training set (NR99,
331	#'     recommended for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`).
332	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species assignment file for
333	#'     `dada2::addSpecies()`.
334	#'   - `"sintax"`: VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX database, converted locally from
335	#'     the dada2 `toSpecies` trainset (7 ranks `d,p,c,o,f,g,s`). Sequence
336	#'     labels are synthetic (`SILVA<version>_<target>_NNNNNN`) because the
337	#'     dada2 trainset carries no accession. Written as a separate
338	#'     `*_sintax.fasta.gz` file.
339	#'   - `"raw"`: raw SILVA NR99 FASTA with taxonomy from arb-silva.de.
340	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
341	#'
342	#' @returns The path to the resulting file (invisibly). For `"sintax"` this
343	#'   is the converted `*_sintax.fasta.gz`; the intermediate trainset is also
344	#'   kept in `dest_dir`.
345	#' @export
346	#' @author Adrien Taudière
347	#' @details
348	#' The dada2-formatted files are provided by arb-silva.de and are the
349	#' recommended format for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()` and
350	#' `dada2::addSpecies()`. See
351	#' <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for details.
352	#'
353	#' SILVA data is free for academic use. Commercial use requires a license.
354	#' See <https://www.arb-silva.de/silva-license-information/>.
355	#'
356	#' Please cite: Quast C et al. (2013) The SILVA ribosomal RNA gene
357	#' database project. Nucleic Acids Research 41:D590-D596.
358	#' \doi{10.1093/nar/gks1219}
359	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()]
360	#' @examples
361	#' \dontrun{
362	#' # Download dada2-formatted SILVA for assignTaxonomy()
363	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases")
364	#'
365	#' # Download species assignment file
366	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "dada2_species")
367	#'
368	#' # Download a SINTAX database for SSU (converted from the dada2 trainset)
369	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "sintax")
370	#'
371	#' # SINTAX database for LSU
372	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", target = "LSU", format = "sintax")
373	#'
374	#' # Download raw SILVA NR99 FASTA
375	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "raw")
376	#' }
377	download_silva_db <- function(
378	  dest_dir = ".",
379	  version = "138.2",
380	  target = c("SSU", "LSU"),
381	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "sintax", "raw"),
382	  verbose = TRUE
383	) {
384	  target <- match.arg(target)
385	  format <- match.arg(format)
386	
387	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
388	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
389	  }
390	
391	  if (format == "raw") {
392	    # Raw SILVA export from arb-silva.de (version dir uses 138.2 -> 138_2)
393	    version_us <- gsub("\\.", "_", version)
394	    target_full <- paste0(target, "Ref_NR99")
395	    filename <- paste0(
396	      "SILVA_",
397	      version,
398	      "_",
399	      target_full,
400	      "_tax_silva_trunc.fasta.gz"
401	    )
402	    url <- paste0(
403	      "https://www.arb-silva.de/fileadmin/silva_databases/",
404	      "release_",
405	      version_us,
406	      "/Exports/",
407	      filename
408	    )
409	    dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
410	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
411	    return(invisible(dest_file))
412	  }
413	
414	  # dada2-formatted training sets from the official arb-silva DADA2 release
415	  # (available for both SSU and LSU). The path segment is the DADA2 tooling
416	  # release; the filename carries the SILVA version.
417	  dada2_tool_version <- "1.36.0"
418	  dada2_base <- paste0(
419	    "https://www.arb-silva.de/fileadmin/silva_databases/current/DADA2/",
420	    dada2_tool_version,
421	    "/",
422	    target,
423	    "/"
424	  )
425	
426	  if (format == "dada2_species") {
427	    filename <- paste0("silva_v", version, "_assignSpecies.fa.gz")
428	  } else {
429	    # "dada2" and "sintax" both start from the toSpecies trainset
430	    filename <- paste0("silva_nr99_v", version, "_toSpecies_trainset.fa.gz")
431	  }
432	  url <- paste0(dada2_base, filename)
433	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
434	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
435	
436	  if (format == "sintax") {
437	    sintax_file <- file.path(
438	      dest_dir,
439	      paste0("silva_nr99_v", version, "_", target, "_sintax.fasta.gz")
440	    )
441	    if (verbose) {
442	      message("Converting dada2 trainset to SINTAX format ...")
443	    }
444	    format2sintax(
445	      fasta_db = dest_file,
446	      input_format = "dada2",
447	      output_path = sintax_file,
448	      id_prefix = paste0("SILVA", version, "_", target, "_")
449	    )
450	    return(invisible(sintax_file))
451	  }
452	
453	  invisible(dest_file)
454	}
455

457	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
458	# PR2
459	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
460	
461	#' Download a PR2 reference database
462	#'
463	#' @description
464	#' Downloads the PR2 protist ribosomal reference database from GitHub
465	#' releases. PR2 provides 18S rRNA gene sequences for protists and other
466	#' eukaryotes.
467	#'
468	#' For more advanced access to PR2 data (e.g., full taxonomy tables,
469	#' metadata, or custom queries), see the
470	#' [pr2database](https://pr2database.github.io/pr2database/) R package.
471	#'
472	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
473	#'   downloaded file.
474	#' @param version (Character) PR2 version number (e.g., `"5.0.0"`). If
475	#'   `NULL` (default), the latest release is fetched from GitHub.
476	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of `"dada2"`, `"mothur"`,
477	#'   `"UTAX"`, or `"sintax"` (alias for `"UTAX"`).
478	#' @param marker (Character, default `"SSU"`) One of `"SSU"` or `"plastid"`.
479	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
480	#'
481	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
482	#' @export
483	#' @author Adrien Taudière
484	#' @details
485	#' PR2 releases are hosted on GitHub at
486	#' <https://github.com/pr2database/pr2database/releases>.
487	#'
488	#' Please cite: Guillou L et al. (2013) The Protist Ribosomal Reference
489	#' database (PR2). Nucleic Acids Research 41:D1108-D1113.
490	#' \doi{10.1093/nar/gks1160}
491	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()],
492	#'   [pr2database](https://pr2database.github.io/pr2database/) R package
493	#' @examples
494	#' \dontrun{
495	#' # Download latest PR2 in dada2 format
496	#' download_pr2_db(dest_dir = "databases")
497	#'
498	#' # Download specific version in UTAX format
499	#' download_pr2_db(dest_dir = "databases", version = "5.0.0", format = "UTAX")
500	#' }
501	download_pr2_db <- function(
502	  dest_dir = ".",
503	  version = NULL,
504	  format = c("dada2", "mothur", "UTAX", "sintax"),
505	  marker = c("SSU", "plastid"),
506	  verbose = TRUE
507	) {
508	  format <- match.arg(format)
509	  # "sintax" is an alias for "UTAX" (same format, different naming conventions)
510	  if (format == "sintax") {
511	    format <- "UTAX"
512	  }
513	  marker <- match.arg(marker)
514	
515	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
516	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
517	  }
518	
519	  # Get latest version from GitHub API if not specified
520	  if (is.null(version)) {
521	    if (verbose) {
522	      message("Querying GitHub for latest PR2 release...")
523	    }
524	    api_url <- paste0(
525	      "https://api.github.com/repos/pr2database/pr2database/",
526	      "releases/latest"
527	    )
528	    response <- tryCatch(
529	      jsonlite::fromJSON(api_url),
530	      error = function(e) {
531	        stop(
532	          "Could not query GitHub API for latest PR2 version. ",
533	          "Specify version manually (e.g., version = '5.0.0').\n",
534	          "Error: ",
535	          conditionMessage(e),
536	          call. = FALSE
537	        )
538	      }
539	    )
540	    version <- sub("^v", "", response$tag_name)
541	    if (verbose) {
542	      message("Latest PR2 version: ", version)
543	    }
544	  }
545	
546	  # Construct filename
547	  # Pattern: pr2_version_{VERSION}_SSU_dada2.fasta.gz
548	  marker_str <- ifelse(marker == "SSU", "SSU", "plastid_16S")
549	  filename <- paste0(
550	    "pr2_version_",
551	    version,
552	    "_",
553	    marker_str,
554	    "_",
555	    format,
556	    ".fasta.gz"
557	  )
558	
559	  url <- paste0(
560	    "https://github.com/pr2database/pr2database/releases/download/",
561	    "v",
562	    version,
563	    "/",
564	    filename
565	  )
566	
567	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
568	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
569	
570	  invisible(dest_file)
571	}
572

574	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
575	# BOLD
576	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
577	
578	#' Download sequences from BOLD Systems
579	#'
580	#' @description
581	#' Downloads reference sequences from BOLD Systems (Barcode of Life Data)
582	#' for a given taxonomic group. Unlike other databases, BOLD does not
583	#' provide a single pre-built reference FASTA — sequences are queried by
584	#' taxon via the BOLD API.
585	#'
586	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
587	#'   downloaded file.
588	#' @param taxon (Character, required) Taxonomic name to query (e.g.,
589	#'   `"Fungi"`, `"Arthropoda"`, `"Mammalia"`).
590	#' @param marker (Character, default `"COI-5P"`) The barcode marker. Common
591	#'   values: `"COI-5P"`, `"ITS"`, `"matK"`, `"rbcL"`.
592	#' @param output_format (Character, default `"fasta"`) Output format.
593	#'   Currently only `"fasta"` is supported.
594	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
595	#'
596	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
597	#' @export
598	#' @author Adrien Taudière
599	#' @details
600	#' This function uses the BOLD v3 public API. For very large taxonomic
601	#' groups, the download may take a long time or fail due to server limits.
602	#' In such cases, consider using narrower taxonomic queries or the
603	#' [bold](https://docs.ropensci.org/bold/) R package.
604	#'
605	#' Please cite: Ratnasingham S & Hebert PDN (2007) BOLD: The Barcode of
606	#' Life Data System. Molecular Ecology Notes 7:355-364.
607	#' \doi{10.1111/j.1471-8286.2007.01678.x}
608	#' @examples
609	#' \dontrun{
610	#' # Download COI sequences for Fungi
611	#' download_bold_db(dest_dir = "databases", taxon = "Fungi")
612	#'
613	#' # Download ITS sequences for a specific order
614	#' download_bold_db(
615	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
616	#'   taxon = "Agaricales",
617	#'   marker = "ITS"
618	#' )
619	#' }
620	download_bold_db <- function(
621	  dest_dir = ".",
622	  taxon = NULL,
623	  marker = "COI-5P",
624	  output_format = "fasta",
625	  verbose = TRUE
626	) {
627	  if (is.null(taxon)) {
628	    stop("You must specify a taxon (e.g., taxon = 'Fungi').", call. = FALSE)
629	  }
630	
631	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
632	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
633	  }
634	
635	  # BOLD v3 API for sequence downloads
636	  url <- paste0(
637	    "http://v3.boldsystems.org/index.php/API_Public/sequence",
638	    "?taxon=",
639	    utils::URLencode(taxon, reserved = TRUE)
640	  )
641	
642	  filename <- paste0(
643	    "BOLD_",
644	    gsub(" ", "_", taxon),
645	    "_",
646	    marker,
647	    ".fasta"
648	  )
649	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
650	
651	  if (verbose) {
652	    message(
653	      "Querying BOLD API for '",
654	      taxon,
655	      "' sequences...\n",
656	      "Note: large taxonomic groups may take several minutes."
657	    )
658	  }
659	
660	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
661	
662	  # The BOLD API returns all markers; filter by marker if needed
663	  if (verbose) {
664	    n_seq <- count_pattern_db(dest_file, pattern = ">")
665	    message("Downloaded ", n_seq, " sequences for '", taxon, "'")
666	    message(
667	      "Note: BOLD API returns all markers. You may want to filter ",
668	      "for '",
669	      marker,
670	      "' using filter_db()."
671	    )
672	  }
673	
674	  invisible(dest_file)
675	}
676

678	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
679	# MaarjAM
680	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
681	
682	#' Download the MaarjAM reference database
683	#'
684	#' @description
685	#' Downloads the MaarjAM database of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (AMF)
686	#' virtual taxa (VT) sequences (18S rDNA). The database is maintained at
687	#' the University of Tartu.
688	#'
689	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
690	#'   downloaded file.
691	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL for the MaarjAM FASTA file.
692	#'   Defaults to the known download endpoint. Override if the URL has
693	#'   changed.
694	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
695	#'
696	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
697	#' @export
698	#' @author Adrien Taudière
699	#' @details
700	#' MaarjAM does not have a versioned API. The download URL may change.
701	#' If the default URL fails, visit <https://maarjam.ut.ee/?action=bDownload>
702	#' to find the current download link and pass it via the `url` parameter.
703	#'
704	#' Please cite: Opik M et al. (2010) The online database MaarjAM reveals
705	#' global and ecosystemic distribution patterns in arbuscular mycorrhizal
706	#' fungi (Glomeromycota). New Phytologist 188:223-241.
707	#' \doi{10.1111/j.1469-8137.2010.03334.x}
708	#' @examples
709	#' \dontrun{
710	#' download_marjaam_db(dest_dir = "databases")
711	#' }
712	download_marjaam_db <- function(
713	  dest_dir = ".",
714	  url = "https://maarjam.ut.ee/resources/maarjam_database.fasta",
715	  verbose = TRUE
716	) {
717	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
718	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
719	  }
720	
721	  filename <- "maarjam_database.fasta"
722	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
723	
724	  tryCatch(
725	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose),
726	    error = function(e) {
727	      stop(
728	        "MaarjAM download failed. The URL may have changed.\n",
729	        "Visit https://maarjam.ut.ee/?action=bDownload to find the ",
730	        "current download link, then use:\n",
731	        "  download_marjaam_db(url = 'https://...')\n",
732	        "Original error: ",
733	        conditionMessage(e),
734	        call. = FALSE
735	      )
736	    }
737	  )
738	
739	  invisible(dest_file)
740	}
741

743	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
744	# Eukaryome
745	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
746	
747	#' Download the Eukaryome reference database
748	#'
749	#' @description
750	#' Downloads the Eukaryome database for eukaryotic organisms. Supports
751	#' multiple markers (SSU 18S, ITS, LSU 28S) and output formats (general
752	#' FASTA, dada2, mothur).
753	#'
754	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
755	#'   downloaded file.
756	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL for the Eukaryome file.
757	#'   Override if the URL has changed. If `NULL` (default), the function
758	#'   directs you to the Eukaryome download page.
759	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
760	#'
761	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly), or a message
762	#'   with download instructions if no URL is provided.
763	#' @export
764	#' @author Adrien Taudière
765	#' @details
766	#' Eukaryome does not provide a stable programmatic download API. Files
767	#' are available at <https://eukaryome.org/download/> in several formats:
768	#'
769	#' - General FASTA: <https://eukaryome.org/generalfasta/>
770	#' - dada2 format: <https://eukaryome.org/dada2/>
771	#' - mothur format: <https://eukaryome.org/mothur/>
772	#' - SINTAX format: <https://eukaryome.org/sintax/>
773	#' - QIIME2 format: <https://eukaryome.org/qiime2/>
774	#'
775	#' Visit one of these pages, copy the direct download link, and pass it
776	#' via the `url` parameter.
777	#'
778	#' Please cite: Vasar M et al. (2024) Eukaryome: the rRNA gene reference
779	#' database for identification of all eukaryotes. Database.
780	#' \doi{10.1093/database/baae043}
781	#' @examples
782	#' \dontrun{
783	#' # Download with a specific URL from the Eukaryome website
784	#' download_eukaryome_db(
785	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
786	#'   url = "https://eukaryome.org/files/eukaryome_v1.9_SSU_dada2.fasta.gz"
787	#' )
788	#' }
789	download_eukaryome_db <- function(
790	  dest_dir = ".",
791	  url = NULL,
792	  verbose = TRUE
793	) {
794	  if (is.null(url)) {
795	    message(
796	      "Eukaryome does not provide stable programmatic download URLs.\n",
797	      "Please visit one of these pages to get the download link:\n",
798	      "  - General FASTA: https://eukaryome.org/generalfasta/\n",
799	      "  - dada2 format:  https://eukaryome.org/dada2/\n",
800	      "  - mothur format: https://eukaryome.org/mothur/\n",
801	      "  - SINTAX format: https://eukaryome.org/sintax/\n",
802	      "  - QIIME2 format: https://eukaryome.org/qiime2/\n\n",
803	      "Then call:\n",
804	      "  download_eukaryome_db(url = 'https://...')"
805	    )
806	    return(invisible(NULL))
807	  }
808	
809	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
810	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
811	  }
812	
813	  filename <- basename(url)
814	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
815	
816	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
817	
818	  invisible(dest_file)
819	}
820	
821	
822	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
823	# Greengenes2
824	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
825	
826	#' Download a Greengenes2 reference database
827	#'
828	#' @description
829	#' Downloads the Greengenes2 16S rRNA database. By default, downloads the
830	#' dada2-formatted training sets from Zenodo (maintained by Benjamin
831	#' Callahan). Alternatively, downloads backbone sequences from the
832	#' Greengenes2 FTP server.
833	#'
834	#' Note that Greengenes2 uses `d__` (domain) instead of `k__` (kingdom)
835	#' as the first rank prefix. Use `tax_format = "greengenes2"` with
836	#' [summarize_db()] and [list_ranks_db()] for correct parsing.
837	#'
838	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
839	#'   downloaded file.
840	#' @param version (Character, default `"2024.09"`) Greengenes2 version
841	#'   in `YYYY.MM` format.
842	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
843	#'   - `"dada2"`: dada2-formatted training set from Zenodo (recommended
844	#'     for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`).
845	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species-level training set for
846	#'     `dada2::assignTaxonomy()` (includes species).
847	#'   - `"fasta"`: plain FASTA sequences from the FTP server.
848	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
849	#'
850	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
851	#' @export
852	#' @author Adrien Taudière
853	#' @details
854	#' The dada2-formatted files are maintained by Benjamin Callahan on Zenodo
855	#' and are the same source as the SILVA dada2 training sets. See
856	#' <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for details.
857	#'
858	#' Please cite: McDonald D et al. (2024) Greengenes2 unifies microbial
859	#' data in a single reference tree. Nature Biotechnology 42:715-718.
860	#' \doi{10.1038/s41587-023-01845-1}
861	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [tax_prefixes()]
862	#' @examples
863	#' \dontrun{
864	#' # Download dada2-formatted Greengenes2
865	#' download_greengenes2_db(dest_dir = "databases")
866	#'
867	#' # Download plain FASTA from FTP
868	#' download_greengenes2_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "fasta")
869	#' }
870	download_greengenes2_db <- function(
871	  dest_dir = ".",
872	  version = "2024.09",
873	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "fasta"),
874	  verbose = TRUE
875	) {
876	  format <- match.arg(format)
877	
878	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
879	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
880	  }
881	
882	  # Zenodo record IDs for dada2-formatted Greengenes2
883	  zenodo_records <- list(
884	    "2024.09" = "14169078"
885	  )
886	
887	  version_us <- gsub("\\.", "_", version)
888	
889	  if (format %in% c("dada2", "dada2_species")) {
890	    record_id <- zenodo_records[[version]]
891	    if (is.null(record_id)) {
892	      stop(
893	        "No known Zenodo record for Greengenes2 v",
894	        version,
895	        ". ",
896	        "Known versions: ",
897	        paste(names(zenodo_records), collapse = ", "),
898	        ". Visit https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html",
899	        call. = FALSE
900	      )
901	    }
902	
903	    if (format == "dada2") {
904	      filename <- paste0("gg2_", version_us, "_toGenus_trainset.fa.gz")
905	    } else {
906	      filename <- paste0("gg2_", version_us, "_toSpecies_trainset.fa.gz")
907	    }
908	
909	    url <- paste0(
910	      "https://zenodo.org/records/",
911	      record_id,
912	      "/files/",
913	      filename
914	    )
915	  } else {
916	    # Plain FASTA from Greengenes2 FTP
917	    filename <- paste0(version, ".seqs.fna.gz")
918	    url <- paste0(
919	      "https://ftp.microbio.me/greengenes_release/",
920	      version,
921	      "/",
922	      filename
923	    )
924	  }
925	
926	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
927	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
928	
929	  invisible(dest_file)
930	}
931	
932	
933	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
934	# RDP
935	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
936	
937	#' Download an RDP reference database
938	#'
939	#' @description
940	#' Downloads the Ribosomal Database Project (RDP) 16S rRNA database.
941	#' By default, downloads the dada2-formatted training sets from Zenodo
942	#' (maintained by Benjamin Callahan).
943	#'
944	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
945	#'   downloaded file.
946	#' @param trainset (Character, default `"19"`) RDP trainset version number.
947	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
948	#'   - `"dada2"`: training set for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`.
949	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species assignment file for
950	#'     `dada2::addSpecies()`.
951	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
952	#'
953	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
954	#' @export
955	#' @author Adrien Taudière
956	#' @details
957	#' The dada2-formatted files are maintained by Benjamin Callahan on
958	#' Zenodo. See <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for
959	#' details.
960	#'
961	#' Please cite: Cole JR et al. (2014) Ribosomal Database Project: data
962	#' and tools for high throughput rRNA analysis. Nucleic Acids Research
963	#' 42:D633-D642. \doi{10.1093/nar/gkt1244}
964	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [download_silva_db()]
965	#' @examples
966	#' \dontrun{
967	#' # Download RDP trainset 19 for assignTaxonomy()
968	#' download_rdp_db(dest_dir = "databases")
969	#'
970	#' # Download species assignment file
971	#' download_rdp_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "dada2_species")
972	#' }
973	download_rdp_db <- function(
974	  dest_dir = ".",
975	  trainset = "19",
976	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species"),
977	  verbose = TRUE
978	) {
979	  format <- match.arg(format)
980	
981	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
982	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
983	  }
984	
985	  # Zenodo record IDs for dada2-formatted RDP
986	  zenodo_records <- list(
987	    "19" = "14168771",
988	    "18" = "4310151",
989	    "16" = "801828"
990	  )
991	
992	  record_id <- zenodo_records[[trainset]]
993	  if (is.null(record_id)) {
994	    stop(
995	      "No known Zenodo record for RDP trainset ",
996	      trainset,
997	      ". ",
998	      "Known trainsets: ",
999	      paste(names(zenodo_records), collapse = ", "),
1000	      ". Visit https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html",
1001	      call. = FALSE
1002	    )
1003	  }
1004	
1005	  if (format == "dada2") {
1006	    filename <- paste0("rdp_train_set_", trainset, ".fa.gz")
1007	  } else {
1008	    filename <- paste0("rdp_species_assignment_", trainset, ".fa.gz")
1009	  }
1010	
1011	  url <- paste0(
1012	    "https://zenodo.org/records/",
1013	    record_id,
1014	    "/files/",
1015	    filename
1016	  )
1017	
1018	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
1019	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
1020	
1021	  invisible(dest_file)
1022	}
1023	
1024	
1025	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1026	# MIDORI2
1027	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1028	
1029	#' Download a MIDORI2 reference database
1030	#'
1031	#' @description
1032	#' Downloads the MIDORI2 reference database for eukaryotic mitochondrial
1033	#' genes (COI, 12S, 16S, Cytb, etc.). MIDORI2 provides pre-formatted
1034	#' FASTA files for multiple classifiers (dada2, SINTAX, RDP, BLAST).
1035	#'
1036	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
1037	#'   downloaded file.
1038	#' @param gene (Character, default `"CO1"`) Mitochondrial gene marker.
1039	#'   Common values: `"CO1"`, `"srRNA"` (12S), `"lrRNA"` (16S), `"Cytb"`.
1040	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of `"dada2"`,
1041	#'   `"dada2_species"`, `"SINTAX"`, `"RDP"`, or `"BLAST"`.
1042	#' @param seq_type (Character, default `"UNIQ"`) One of `"UNIQ"` (all
1043	#'   unique haplotypes per species) or `"LONGEST"` (single longest
1044	#'   sequence per species).
1045	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL. If `NULL` (default), the
1046	#'   function provides instructions and the download page URL.
1047	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
1048	#'
1049	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly), or a message
1050	#'   with download instructions if no URL is provided.
1051	#' @export
1052	#' @author Adrien Taudière
1053	#' @details
1054	#' MIDORI2 download URLs include a date-stamped directory path that
1055	#' changes with each GenBank release, making fully programmatic access
1056	#' fragile. Visit <https://www.reference-midori.info/download.php> to
1057	#' find the current download URL for your desired gene and format, then
1058	#' pass it via the `url` parameter.
1059	#'
1060	#' Files are typically named following this pattern:
1061	#' `MIDORI2_{TYPE}_NUC_SP_GB{VERSION}_{GENE}_{FORMAT}.fasta.gz`
1062	#'
1063	#' Please cite: Leray M et al. (2022) MIDORI2: A collection of quality
1064	#' controlled, preformatted, and regularly updated reference databases
1065	#' for taxonomic assignment of eukaryotic mitochondrial sequences.
1066	#' Environmental DNA 4:894-907. \doi{10.1002/edn3.303}
1067	#' @seealso [format2sintax()], [format2dada2()]
1068	#' @examples
1069	#' \dontrun{
1070	#' # Get instructions for downloading MIDORI2
1071	#' download_midori2_db()
1072	#'
1073	#' # Download with a specific URL
1074	#' download_midori2_db(
1075	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
1076	#'   url = "https://reference-midori.info/download/Databases/..."
1077	#' )
1078	#' }
1079	download_midori2_db <- function(
1080	  dest_dir = ".",
1081	  gene = "CO1",
1082	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "SINTAX", "RDP", "BLAST"),
1083	  seq_type = c("UNIQ", "LONGEST"),
1084	  url = NULL,
1085	  verbose = TRUE
1086	) {
1087	  format <- match.arg(format)
1088	  seq_type <- match.arg(seq_type)
1089	
1090	  if (is.null(url)) {
1091	    format_dir <- switch(
1092	      format,
1093	      dada2 = "DADA2",
1094	      dada2_species = "DADA2_sp",
1095	      SINTAX = "SINTAX",
1096	      RDP = "RDP",
1097	      BLAST = "BLAST"
1098	    )
1099	    message(
1100	      "MIDORI2 download URLs change with each GenBank release.\n",
1101	      "Please visit the download page to get the URL for your desired ",
1102	      "gene and format:\n",
1103	      "  https://www.reference-midori.info/download.php\n\n",
1104	      "Look for a file matching this pattern:\n",
1105	      "  MIDORI2_",
1106	      seq_type,
1107	      "_NUC_SP_GB{VERSION}_",
1108	      gene,
1109	      "_",
1110	      toupper(format_dir),
1111	      ".fasta.gz\n\n",
1112	      "Then call:\n",
1113	      "  download_midori2_db(url = 'https://...')"
1114	    )
1115	    return(invisible(NULL))
1116	  }
1117	
1118	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
1119	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
1120	  }
1121	
1122	  filename <- basename(url)
1123	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
1124	
1125	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
1126	
1127	  invisible(dest_file)
1128	}
1129

1132	# DIAT.barcode
1133	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1134	
1135	#' Download a Diat.barcode reference database
1136	#'
1137	#' @description
1138	#' Downloads the Diat.barcode reference database for diatom rbcL barcoding.
1139	#' This is a curated database for diatom identification using the rbcL
1140	#' marker gene. The recommended access method is through the
1141	#' [diatbarcode](https://github.com/fkeck/diatbarcode) R package, which
1142	#' provides additional tools for working with the database.
1143	#'
